title: Macroeconomic Analysis of Nicaragua
text:
This project was completed as part of the ECO1010 Macroeconomics module at DCU. The task involved conducting an in-depth analysis of the macroeconomic conditions and policies of an assigned economy, based on the IMF’s Article IV Consultation document.
My assigned economy was Nicaragua. The report covers the country’s current macroeconomic conditions, including GDP growth, inflation, and the balance of payments. It then analyses fiscal policy, public debt sustainability, and the implications of the government’s budgetary decisions. The monetary policy section examines Nicaragua’s crawling peg exchange rate regime, interest rate decisions, and the challenges of monetary transmission in a highly dollarised economy. The final section evaluates future risks, including the potential impact of changes in US migration policies on the Nicaraguan labour market and economy.
This project strengthened my ability to apply macroeconomic models, equations, and diagrams to a real-world economy. It also improved my skills in critical analysis, economic reasoning, and structuring a comprehensive policy-focused report.

title: Business Strategy Case Study: Lyten
text:
This project was completed as part of the BAA1019 Business Strategy module at DCU. The assignment was an open-book case study analysis examining Lyten, a US lithium-sulphur battery start-up that acquired key assets from Northvolt’s bankruptcy, including a gigafactory in Sweden and expansion plans across Germany and Canada.
The analysis addressed three strategic questions: identifying key strategic issues facing Lyten, evaluating its cross-border expansion as either a coherent internationalisation strategy or premature global scaling, and assessing the trade-off between geopolitical alignment and commercial flexibility. The report drew on established strategic frameworks, including Hambrick and Fredrickson’s Strategy Diamond, McKinsey’s Three Horizons, and Teece’s Dynamic Capabilities framework, to critically evaluate the company’s decisions.
This project sharpened my ability to apply strategic management theory to real-world business cases, develop structured analytical arguments, and assess complex cross-border expansion decisions in a rapidly evolving industry.

title: Daniel Vala
text:
Welcome to my ePortfolio!
In this ePortfolio, you can learn more about me, my past experiences, achievements, and interests. You will also find some of my academic projects here, which highlight my learning journey and the areas I am passionate about.
Feel free to explore the website to whatever extent you like, and do not hesitate to reach out if you have any questions.

title: About Me
text:
I am a final-year Accounting and Finance student at Dublin City University, specialising in Data Analytics and Finance. Throughout my studies, I have developed a strong interest in financial markets, investments, and trading, and I enjoy combining analytical thinking with practical decision-making. My academic path has allowed me to build a solid foundation in finance while also strengthening my skills in data analysis, financial modelling, and problem-solving.
My interest in finance began at an early age, when I started investing at 15 and became increasingly curious about how markets work. Since then, I have continued to deepen my understanding of economics and financial markets, both through my university studies and through independent learning. Over time, this interest has grown into a genuine passion, especially in the areas of investing, trading, and market analysis. I am particularly interested in understanding how economic developments influence financial markets and investment decisions.
Alongside my academic background, I have also gained practical experience in professional and customer-facing environments. During my internship at PwC in the Tax Financial Services department, I worked on real client cases, supported financial data analysis, researched tax legislation, and contributed to testing AI tools in practice. This experience helped me improve both my technical and communication skills, while also showing me the importance of working effectively within a professional team.
Before and during university, I also worked in roles that strengthened my communication, adaptability, and responsibility. Working in retail and hospitality taught me how to interact with different people, solve problems independently, and stay effective in fast-paced environments. These experiences have shaped me not only as a student, but also as a person who is motivated, reliable, and always willing to learn.
What motivates me most is continuous growth — both personal and professional. Moving to Ireland to study, adapting to a new environment, and pursuing strong academic results have all pushed me to develop resilience and ambition. Through this ePortfolio, I would like to share more about my background, achievements, interests, and the projects that reflect my journey so far.

title: Achievements
text: Throughout my academic and personal journey, I have had the opportunity to participate in several competitions and earn recognition for my work. Below are some of the achievements I am most proud of.

title: Achievements
text:
Economics Olympiad






13th place in the National Final | 2022
In 2022, I competed in the Economics Olympiad in the Czech Republic, a prestigious competition running across 22 countries. After passing both the school and regional rounds, I reached the National Final hosted by the Czech National Bank, where I placed 13th out of over 30,000 competitors nationwide. This experience deepened my understanding of economics and confirmed my passion for the field.

title: Achievements
text:
Secondary School Research Competition






4th place in Moravia | 2022
In the same year, I achieved 4th place in the Moravian round of the Secondary School Research Competition (Středoškolská Odborná Činnost). My project focused on the economic aspects of Bitcoin and its comparison to fiat currencies. As part of the research, I conducted interviews with two leading Czech economists and defended my findings in front of an expert jury.

title: Achievements
section: Trinity SMF x Rotman National Market Challenge
text:
Trinity SMF x Rotman National Market Challenge






7th place in Ireland | 2025
In 2025, I took part in the Rotman Market Simulation Challenge hosted by Trinity SMF in Dublin. Together with my teammate, we placed 7th in Ireland, competing in a fast-paced trading simulation that tested our understanding of market behaviour and ability to make split-second decisions. The experience gave me valuable insights into institutional trading dynamics.

title: Achievements
text:
PwC Award for Financial Accounting






Top 10 in class | 2024
In 2024, I was recognised with the PwC Award for my performance in the Financial Accounting module at DCU, placing in the top 10 in my class. This achievement reflects my dedication to understanding financial reporting standards, IFRS, and the preparation of financial statements.

title: FinTech Product Pitch: StepStone
text:
This project was completed as part of the EF308 Econometrics and Forecasting module at DCU. The assignment challenged students to conceptualise a financial advisory FinTech product and support its launch with data analytics.
I developed StepStone, a concept for a mobile app designed to help Irish university students progress toward long-term financial goals, such as buying their first home or building an investment portfolio. The project was built around a survey dataset of over 1,000 Irish university students, capturing demographics, employment, income, spending behaviour, and savings.
The data analysis, conducted in Python, covered the full analytical pipeline: data cleaning and validation, MCAR testing for missing data, outlier inspection, exploratory data analysis comparing students living at home versus independently, regression modelling, and forecasting. The project was accompanied by a video pitch presenting the product idea and key findings from the analysis.
This project developed my skills in econometric analysis, Python programming, data-driven storytelling, and presenting technical findings to a non-technical audience through video.
